title: A prenatal skin atlas reveals immune regulation of human skin morphogenesis
authors: Gopee N, Winheim E, Olabi B, et al.
summary: Comprehensive multi-omics atlas of human prenatal skin (7-17 PCW). Pericytes are annotated as a fine-grained population under the broad Mural cell category. Mural cells co-locate with endothelial and neural cells in vascular microenvironments. LYVE1+ macrophages interact with vascular cells through PDGF signalling. Pericytes/mural cells had a low probability of correspondence to adult skin cell states even after 19 weeks of SkO culture.
quotes: macrophage subsets (Extended Data Fig. 7e,f) were predicted to co-locate with fibroblasts, neural cells and vascular cells in distinct tissue microenvironments in early gestation

LYVE1+ macrophages co-located with WNT2+ fibroblasts and were predicted to interact through platelet-derived growth factors (PDGFs) and corresponding receptors (PDGFRα and PDGFRβ) expressed on fibroblasts
Mesoderm-derived cells, including skin fibroblasts and endothelial cells, and immune cells were present throughout gestation
Visium deconvolution analysis predicted co-location of prenatal skin macrophages with endothelial cells ('early and late neurovascular microenvironments', ME1 and ME5)
immune cells are required to fully recapitulate in vivo endothelial cell development
